name: Madeira Gravy
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Madeira wine or dry sherry
recipeInstructions:
1. Pour the Madeira wine into a small saucepan and bring it to a boil, uncovered.
2. Reduce the wine by about half.
3. Strain the pan juices from roasted turkey into a 2-cup glass measure, gently pressing on the solids to extract as much liquid as possible.
4. Allow the juices to rest for a few minutes, then skim away any floating grease.
5. Add chicken broth to the strained juices until the total liquid measures 1 1/2 cups.
6. Combine the juice-broth mixture with the reduced wine, adding pepper and thyme.
7. Bring the gravy to a boil for a minute or two.
8. Remove from heat and serve with turkey. Makes approximately 16 servings.
Approximate nutritional analysis: 18 calories per 2-tablespoon serving; 1 gram carbohydrate; .3 grams fat (13% of calories); 80 milligrams sodium.
American Health -- November 1995
